channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Are government-paid media subscriptions a 'scandal'? That's what Donald Trump is now asserting
item.description: Federal government payments for subscriptions to media outlets have become the latest target of President Donald Trump's spending hawks.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump meets with congressional Republicans as GOP lawmakers argue over tax and spending cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted an unusually long meeting with House Republicans at the White House on Thursday, turning over prime workspace for them to hammer out differences over the size, scope and details of their multi-trillion plan to cut taxes, regulations and government spen
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers advance a bill to prevent transgender women from competing in women's sports
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A day after Georgia Lt. Gov. Burt Jones stood with President Donald Trump in Washington as he signed an executive order intended to prevent transgender athletes from competing in women's sports, he returned to Georgia where state Senate Republicans made a similar move.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump's federal worker buyout plan could impact San Diego
item.description: As the deadline looms for workers to accept the Trump administration's sweeping separation offer, the more than two million civilian employees by the federal government across the country are weighing whether to take it.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump unveils sanctions against International Criminal Court for Netanyahu arrest warrant
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order issuing financial sanctions and visa restrictions against International Criminal Court officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump pushes the limits of presidential power, the courts push back
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A familiar pattern has emerged since President Donald Trump returned to the White House less than three weeks ago: He makes a brash proposal, his opponents file a lawsuit and a federal judge puts the plan on hold.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2nd federal judge in 2 days blocks Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A second federal judge in two days has blocked President Donald Trump’s effort to end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, decrying what he described as the administration's attempt to ignore the Constitution for political gain. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom changes tone on Trump from campaign rhetoric with federal wildfire recovery funds at stake
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has long been a leading adversary and target of President Donald Trump. But the governor, in need of federal help, is working with the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More of Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nominations process for President Donald Trump's appointees is picking up pace, with some confirmed by the Senate and hearings scheduled for more of his Cabinet picks. Some are already at work.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Judge temporarily blocks Trump plan offering incentives for federal workers to resign
item.description: A federal judge has blocked President Donald Trump’s plan to push out federal employees by offering them financial incentives.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is 'ordo amoris?' Vice President JD Vance invokes this medieval Catholic concept
item.description: Vice President JD Vance recently cited medieval Catholic theology in justifying the immigration crackdown under President Donald Trump. "Just google ‘ordo amoris,’” he posted Jan. 30 on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defense minister tells army to prepare to relocate Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: He says the military should prepare exit options by land, sea and air so that anyone who wishes to can leave for "any country willing to accept them.
item.dc:creator: Jerome Socolovsky and Ryland Barton, MPR News

item.title: As 'Fork' resignation deadline arrives, a federal judge will consider a stay
item.description: The Trump administration has given more than 2 million federal employees until Thursday to decide whether to stay or go. A last-minute court hearing may throw a wrench into the administration's plans.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Protesters rally against Trump's stop-work order for USAID
item.description: USAID and nonprofit workers gathered near the Capitol in D.C. to protest Elon Musk's efforts to shut down the aid agency with his Department of Government Efficiency, or DOGE.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Renters' rights and 13 more bills to watch at the 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Lawmakers are busy in Carson City drawing up new rules to live by, including tougher penalties for committing crimes, protections for transgender Nevadans and a variety of laws related to housing and renters' rights.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump unleashes a supercharged MAGA agenda and Republicans come aboard
item.description: President Donald Trump is testing how far Republicans are willing to go in supporting his supercharged “Make America Great Again” agenda, tearing down government agencies and taking actions fundamentally at odds with once-traditional party principles.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Education a key focus for lawmakers, DeSantis in 2025 state budget
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis is urging lawmakers to consider his state budget proposal one month before they are back in Tallahassee to begin the 2025 legislative session.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic senators protest after they say Trump gives Musk's staff access to classified info
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats on the Senate Intelligence Committee are demanding answers after they say President Donald Trump gave billionaire Elon Musk and his staff access to sensitive data and classified secrets as part of their work to overhaul the federal government.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Catholic aid group downsizes by nearly 50% amid USAID freeze
item.description: The group started shutting down programs funded by USAID, which supplied about half of its $1.5 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls‘ and women‘s sports
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order designed to prevent transgender athletes from participating in girls’ or women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Stauber reintroduces bill to reverse mining ban near Boundary Waters, return leases to Twin Metals
item.description: Northern Minnesota Republican Congressman Pete Stauber has reintroduced legislation to overturn a 20-year mining ban near the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness. If passed, the bill would also return mineral leases to Twin Metals, the company vying to build an underground copper mine near Ely.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Freshman Democrat, physician Rep. Morrison warns against RFK Jr. nomination
item.description: Minnesota Third-District Democratic Rep. Kelly Morrison, who is one of a handful of physicians in Congress, joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to express her concerns and what Kennedy’s confirmation could mean in practice.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: The first group of migrants has been sent to Guantánamo, but legal challenges loom
item.description: President Trump aims to deport up to 30,000 migrant detainees to a holding facility there, despite the questionable legality of that move.
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer, MPR News

item.title: Texas bill that lets families use tax dollars for private schools swiftly sails through Senate
item.description: Senate Bill 2 now heads to the House, where top officials say they have the support to create education savings accounts.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Homeland Security's workforce might not be as big as Trump's immigration goals
item.description: Changes to hiring and retention practices grew the department by 19,000 people in four years. But former officials warn that's still not enough for Trump's ambitious policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bill count swells to 484 as Nevada Legislature hits Day 2; Voter ID a Republican goal
item.description: Packed binders. Busy halls. Lobbyists, lawmakers and journalists roaming amongst each other. This is when the routine of the state government sets in. Already, there are 484 bills submitted this session, with many more expected.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5
